message:
fix(frankenlibc-abi/string_abi): pass allocation bytes_remaining to maybe_clamp_copy_len instead of the raw pointer address

The clamp sites in `memchr`, `memrchr`, and `strncpy` were constructing the
`src_remaining` / `dst_remaining` arguments to `maybe_clamp_copy_len` as
`Some(ptr as usize)`. That signature expects an `Option<usize>` whose inner
value is **bytes remaining in the allocation** (as produced by
`known_remaining`), not the raw pointer address. Passing the address meant:

  * For pointers into a real allocation, the clamp computation was fed a
    large, meaningless integer (the virtual address of the buffer), so the
    "how many bytes are left?" check became a no-op and repair mode never
    actually trimmed `n` to the true allocation size.
  * For raw pointers with no known allocation, the code fabricated a
    `Some(addr)` instead of returning `None`, which also defeated the
    "unknown - bail out conservatively" branch inside `maybe_clamp_copy_len`.

Routed both arguments through `crate::malloc_abi::known_remaining(..)`
(already imported at line 23), matching the pattern used throughout
`wchar_abi.rs`, `resolv_abi.rs`, and the rest of `string_abi.rs`. This
restores the intended repair semantics: when the allocation tracker knows
the remaining size, `memchr`/`memrchr`/`strncpy` now correctly clamp to
that size under repair mode; when it doesn't, they fall through to the
existing `None` path.

Only the three call sites changed; `maybe_clamp_copy_len` itself and the
`known_remaining` helper are untouched. No public API changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
